claim: The 26-29% vs 75% discrepancy between Brain-V's reconciled crust-only fraction measurement and Stolfi's published figure is explainable by one or more of: (1) EVA transcription-version drift between Stolfi's 2000 source and Brain-V's 2023-2025 ZL3b transcription; (2) a 'normal words' filter that Stolfi applied but Brain-V has not; (3) a sub-corpus restriction (by hand, by section) that Stolfi used implicitly. If one of these resolves the gap to within ±5% of 75%, the discrepancy is diagnosed. If none do, it is a genuine open question of corpus evolution or unstated Stolfi methodology.
evidence_for: H-BV-RECONCILED-RERUN-01 T1 confirmed the crust-only fraction at 26-29% on the whole manuscript under the reconciled config, and at similar values on Hand A (29%) and Hand B (29%) alone. The gap is stable across hands — not A-specific, not a filter-choice artefact at the q-initial level.

Stolfi's 2000 paper predates the IVTFF EvaT transcription Brain-V uses (ZL3b of 2025-05-13). Stolfi would likely have used either a Currier-direct transcription or an early EVA variant. EVA transcriptions are known to differ in how certain ambiguous glyph sequences are segmented, which can shift the core/mantle glyph tallies.

Stolfi explicitly describes a 'normal words' class and excludes 'weirdos' from his grammar analysis: '1295 tokens (3.7%) in the main text, 127 tokens (12.4%) in the labels'. Brain-V does not currently apply a 'weirdos' filter. If weirdos disproportionately contain core/mantle glyphs, excluding them would raise the crust-only fraction.

Stolfi notes the paradigm has 'fairly uniform fit over all sections of the manuscript', but 'fairly uniform' is not 'exactly uniform'. The 75% figure may be a weighted mean that differs materially from specific sub-corpora; identifying the highest-fraction section is informative regardless of whether 75% reproduces.
evidence_against: The three Brain-V transcription files (IT2a, RF1b, ZL3b) are all IVTFF Eva variants and likely share core/mantle assignments for the great majority of glyphs. Cross-version drift may be small (<5% absolute) and insufficient to close a 46-percentage-point gap.

Stolfi's 'weirdos' are explicitly 3.7% of main-text tokens. Excluding them from a 37,989-token denominator shifts the fraction by at most 3.7 / (1 - 0.037) = ~3.8 percentage points. Even if all weirdos were split-crust (none crust-only), excluding them can raise the fraction from 26% to ~27%, not to 75%. This sub-test alone cannot explain the gap.

Section-scope restriction may raise or lower the fraction by a few percentage points per section, but for a homogeneous manuscript, the variation across sections should not span the 26-75% range that would be required for this sub-test to close the gap alone.

If the multi-source cumulative also fails to reach 75% ± 5%, the explanation may be that Stolfi's 75% is simply an approximation stated imprecisely in his narrative, and the actual reproducible figure on any reasonable corpus is 26-30%. This outcome is not resolvable without Stolfi's source data or detailed methodology beyond what the grammar page publishes.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Voynich corpus files in raw/corpus/ (IT2a-n.txt, RF1b-e.txt, ZL3b-n.txt) and parsed voynich-corpus.json.

  TOKENISATION (LOCKED): MULTI = ['cth', 'cph', 'ckh', 'cfh', 'ch', 'sh', 'ee'], longest-match-first. Stolfi-literal.
    CORE   = {t, p, k, f, cth, cph, ckh, cfh}
    MANTLE = {ch, sh, ee}
    NON_STANDARD_GLYPHS = {b, c, j, u, v, z} (glyphs that do not appear in Stolfi's layer definitions)

  CRUST-ONLY DEFINITION: a token is 'crust-only' if it contains zero CORE glyphs and zero MANTLE glyphs (after tokenisation).

  STOLFI TARGET: 0.75. Tolerance: 0.05.  PASS for any variant iff |variant_fraction - 0.75| <= 0.05.

  SUB-TEST 1 — TRANSCRIPTION VERSION COMPARISON:
    Parse each of the three IVTFF files in raw/corpus/ using a uniform minimal parser:
      - Strip IVTFF metadata annotations that are NOT primary glyph content:
        {n;} inline markers like @222;, @152; (replace with '?')
        braced alternatives {xxx} (keep first option as primary)
        brackets [x:y] (keep first option x as primary)
        comma-separated alternatives 'a,b' (keep 'a' as primary)
        apostrophe-contraction notation like {c'y} (treat as 'cy')
      - Split on '.' into words; discard empty strings
      - All tokens across all folios form the corpus for that file
    Compute crust_only_fraction under the Brain-V tokenisation and CORE/MANTLE sets.
    For this sub-test, apply no filters beyond the parsing normalisation.
    REPORT: crust_only_fraction per file (IT2a, RF1b, ZL3b). Largest single delta between any two variants is the transcription-version drift magnitude.
    VARIANT CHECK: does any variant's fraction fall in [0.70, 0.80]?

  SUB-TEST 2 — NORMAL-WORDS FILTER AUDIT:
    Starting from the parsed voynich-corpus.json (ZL3b), apply each candidate filter (cumulative and singly):
      F1: exclude hapaxes (tokens with corpus frequency == 1)
      F2: exclude tokens containing any NON_STANDARD_GLYPH
      F3: exclude short tokens (length < 3 glyph-units after tokenisation)
      F4: exclude 'weirdos' — operationalised as tokens that fail to parse under the Stolfi layer grammar, proxied by: tokens with multiple CORE glyphs AND/OR tokens where CORE appears before any MANTLE or CRUST glyph at a non-expected position. LOCKED proxy: 'weirdos' = tokens with >=2 CORE glyphs OR length >=12 glyph-units.
      F5: exclude labels (tokens on label lines — marked in the IVTFF format with @P0 positional codes or by line-position metadata)
    Apply each filter singly (F1-alone, F2-alone, ..., F5-alone) and cumulatively (F1+F2+F3+F4+F5).
    REPORT: crust_only_fraction per filter variant.
    VARIANT CHECK: does any single filter OR the cumulative reach [0.70, 0.80]?

  SUB-TEST 3 — SUB-CORPUS SCOPE:
    From parsed voynich-corpus.json, partition tokens by currier_language and by section:
      Currier partitions: A only, B only, '?' (neither) only
      Section partitions: herbal, astronomical, biological, cosmological, pharmaceutical, recipes, zodiac, text-only
      Cross partitions: herbal-A, herbal-B, pharma-B, biological-B (most common hand-section combinations)
    Compute crust_only_fraction for each partition with no filters beyond the standard.
    REPORT: full table.
    VARIANT CHECK: does any sub-corpus reach [0.70, 0.80]?

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    DIAGNOSED_SINGLE_SOURCE: ANY single variant across all three sub-tests has |fraction - 0.75| <= 0.05. Identify the variant; that is the reconciling choice.
    DIAGNOSED_MULTI_SOURCE: no single variant reaches the target, but the cumulative (transcription = best; all filters applied; narrowest sub-corpus that still has >= 500 tokens) reaches [0.70, 0.80].
    UNRESOLVED_OPEN_QUESTION: neither single-source nor cumulative reaches the target. v2 documents T1 as an unresolved Brain-V-vs-Stolfi discrepancy, with the three investigated-and-ruled-out hypotheses explicitly listed.

  REPORTING:
    For each sub-test: full variant table with fractions. Identify the closest-to-target variant. Report the verdict per the locked rule. Apply methodology lesson 3 implicitly — no external baseline is being newly compared against without sanity check; the 75% itself is the target under investigation.

  WORKED EXAMPLE (to lock sub-test 1 parsing):
    ZL3b line: '<f1r.1,@P0>       <%>fachys.ykal.ar.ataiin.shol.shory.[cth:oto]res.y.kor.sholdy<!@254;>'
    Parse: strip <%>, <!@254;>, then strip [cth:oto] -> keep 'cth' as primary. Result: 'fachys.ykal.ar.ataiin.shol.shory.cthres.y.kor.sholdy'
    Split on '.' -> ['fachys', 'ykal', 'ar', 'ataiin', 'shol', 'shory', 'cthres', 'y', 'kor', 'sholdy']
    Each token contributes to the denominator. Tokens 'fachys' (contains 'f' CORE, 'sh' - wait 'fachys' tokenises as ['f','a','ch','y','s']: has 'f' CORE and 'ch' MANTLE, so NOT crust-only). 'ykal' = ['y','k','a','l']: has 'k' CORE, NOT crust-only. 'ar' = ['a','r']: no CORE no MANTLE, crust-only. Etc.

  WORKED EXAMPLE (to lock filter F4 weirdo proxy):
    Token 'shckhey' tokenises as ['sh','ckh','e','y']. Has 'ckh' CORE (1 core). Length 4. Does NOT match weirdo criterion (requires >=2 cores or length>=12). Not a weirdo.
    Token 'otcholkedy' tokenises as ['o','t','ch','o','l','k','e','d','y']. Has 't' CORE and 'k' CORE (2 cores). Matches weirdo criterion (>=2 cores). IS a weirdo under this proxy.
    Token 'cphoaiiniiinolokeedy' (hypothetical long) tokenises to 12+ units. IS a weirdo by length criterion.
test_metric: Three sub-tests, each with multiple variants. For each variant, compute crust_only_fraction and check against [0.70, 0.80]. Closest-to-target variant identified per sub-test.
test_threshold: DIAGNOSED_SINGLE_SOURCE if any variant in [0.70, 0.80]. DIAGNOSED_MULTI_SOURCE if cumulative reaches [0.70, 0.80] but no single does. UNRESOLVED_OPEN_QUESTION otherwise.
tests_remaining: locked_crust_only_investigation: run 3 sub-tests; identify closest variant per sub-test; compute cumulative; classify verdict
reasoning: H-BV-RECONCILED-RERUN-01 revealed T1 (crust-only fraction) as a persistent Brain-V-vs-Stolfi discrepancy not explained by pipeline reconciliation. The sanity check on suffix dealer mean passed, so the reconciled config is correct. The 26-29% crust-only fraction, vs Stolfi's reported 75%, must have a source outside the suffix-level operationalisation: either corpus version, normal-words filter, or sub-corpus scope. This hypothesis tests all three candidate sources with locked variants per sub-test. The test is specifically agnostic about whether the gap WILL close; the decision rule cleanly handles UNRESOLVED as a legitimate outcome. A scope note is included: this is a methodological diagnosis, not a Hand A structural claim — whatever this test shows, it does not affect the findings that survived H-BV-RECONCILED-RERUN-01 (T2 asymmetry, CAVENEY-FINAL-N-01, COMPOSITE-OUTER-01 M3/M5).
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed diagnostic follow-up to H-BV-RECONCILED-RERUN-01 T1 FAIL. Investigates three candidate sources for the persistent crust-only fraction discrepancy.
